formulas.name: Fund Account Principle
formulas.notes: Fund account represents the amount of money available for a specific purpose.

formulas.name: Permanent Fund Principle
formulas.notes: Assets of a permanent fund will not be used for purposes other than that for which it was created.

formulas.name: Non-Permanent Fund Principle
formulas.notes: Assets of a non-permanent fund may be used for purposes other than that for which it was created.

formulas.name: Donations and Subscriptions Principle
formulas.notes: Donations and subscriptions are revenue receipts.

formulas.name: Investments Principle
formulas.notes: Investments are assets of the NPO and are valued at cost or market value whichever is lower.

formulas.name: Depreciation Principle
formulas.notes: Depreciation is provided on assets other than land and buildings.

formulas.name: Accumulated Fund Principle
formulas.notes: The accumulated fund of a NPO is the total of the funds available for its various purposes.
